titleStmt.title: SII 38.141: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 141. (A.R. No. 141 of 1912.) TIRUVOṞṞIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On a fourth pillar of the verandah, round the central shrine, Ādhipurīśvara temple.

summary.p: Damaged at the end. Begins with the meykīrtti, Tirumaṉṉi-vaḷara.

It records that the nagarattār of Tiruvoṟṟiyūr in Puḻal-nāḍu in Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam (a subdivision) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam received thirty Rājarājan-kāśu from Kūttān Gaṇavadi, an official of the army of (śiṟudanap-perundanam mārāyan), Gaṅgaikoṇḍān alias Uttamachōḻamārāyan, to feed a brāhmaṇa, well-versed in vēdas, during the mid-day offerings to the god, Tiruvoṟṟiyūr Mahādēva. The nagarattār undertook the responsibility of measuring out thirty kalam of paddy, as interest by the measure, Rājakēśari, annually at the temple treasury in Tiruvoṟṟiyūr for the above purpose.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tirumaṉ yum poṟcayap pāvaiyuñ cir
yun=tan perunteviyarāki Inpuṟa yuḷ Iṭaituṟaināṭu [ ntu] ṭarvaṉa velippaṭar vanavā
ciyuñ cuḷḷiccūḻmatiḷ ko kaiyum naṇṇaṟkarumuraṇ maṇṇaik kaṭakkamum poruka
ṭaliḻattaraicarta muṭiyumin=tiranāramu n=tenṟicai yiḻamaṇṭala muḻuvatum meṟipaṭaikkeraḷaṉmu
ṟaimaiyiṟ cūṭuṅkulatanamākiyapalarpukaḻ muṭiyuñ ceṅkatirmālaiyuñ caṅkatiravelaitiRead caṅkatirvelait
tol peruṅkāval palpaḻan=tivum ceruviṟ cinavilirupattoru kālaraicukaḷai kaṭṭa paracurāmaṉ
mevaruñ cān=timat tivaraṇ karuti Iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaku muṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimikamuyaṅ
kiyil mutukiṭṭoḷitta cayaciṅkan Aḷapperum pukaḻoṭum piṭipiRead piṭiyil Iraṭṭapāṭiyeḻarai Ila
kkamum navanitikkulapperumalaikaḷum vikkiramavirar cakkara koṭṭamum mutir vaṭavallai matu
ramaṇṭalamum kāmiṭaivaḷai nāmaṇaikkoṇamum veñcina virar pañcap paḷḷiyum pācaṭaippa
ḻaṉan mācuṇitecamum Ayarvil vaṇpūrttiyāti nakaravaiyiṟ can=tiraṉ ṟol kulattin=tirāta
ṉai viḷaiyamarkkaḷattuk kiḷaiyoṭum piṭittup palatanattoṭu niṟaikulat tanakkuvaiyum kiṭ
ṭaruñ ceṟimiḷai Oṭṭaviṣayamum pūcuraṉcera nalkkocalaināṭu n=tanmapālaṉai vemmuṉaiyaḻittu
vaṇṭuṟaicolait taṇṭaputtiyum Iraṇacūraṉai muraṇukatRead muraṇurattākki tikkaṉaikirtti takkaṇalāṭamuṅ kovi
n=tacan=tan māviḻin=toṭat taṅkātacāral vaṅkāḷatecamum toṭu kaṭaṟ caṅkavoṭṭal mahipālaṉai veñcamar vi
ḷar kaḷattañcu vittaruḷi Oṇṭiṟal yāṉaiyum peṇṭir paṇṭāramum nittilar neṭuṅkaṭaluttiralāṭamum
veṟimalar can=tirantittateṟi punal kaṅkaiyum Alaikaṭal naṭuvuḷ palakalañ celuttic caṅkirāmavijayo
ttuṅkapanmanākiya kaṭārattaraicaṉai vākiyam porukaṭaṟ kumpak kariyoṭu makappaṭutturimai
ṟeRead turimaiyiṟpiṟakkiya perunetip piṟakkamu mārttava (nakar)nakar port toḻil vācalil viccātiratoraṇamu
m mottoḷi punamaṇip putavamuṅ kanamaṇik katavum niṟai cīr vijayamuntuṟai nīrp paṉṇai
yumRead paṇṇaiyumvaṉmalaiyuraiyiṟ toṉmalaiyuru māḻkaṭalakaḻ cūḻ māyiruṭiṅkamuṅ kalaṅkāvalviṉai Ila
laṅkācokamuṅ kāppuṟuniṟai punal māppa(e)pāḷamuṅ kāvalam puricai mevilipaṅkamum viḷaippa
ntūṟuṭai vaḷaippantūṟun=talaittakkor pukaḻ kalaittakkolamuṅ tītamar valviṉai mātamaliṅka
muṅ kalāmutuk kaṭun=tiṟalilāmuri tecamu n=tenakka vārpoḻil mānakka vāramu n=toṭukaṭaṟkāval kaṭumuraṭ kaṭā
ramum māpporu taṇṭāṟkoṇṭa kopparakesarivarmmarāna Uṭaiyār śrīrājendracoḷadevarkku yā
ṇṭu 2 10 9 Āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu puḻaṟkoṭṭattu puḻalnāṭṭu tiruvoṟṟiyūr
nakarattom ciṟutanapperun=tanam mārāyan kaṅkaikoṇṭānāna Uttamacoḻa mārāyan perumpaṭainā
yakan kūttan kaṇavati pakkal koṇṭa [rājarā]jaṉ kācu muppatu Ikkācu muppatuṅ koṇṭu tiruvoṟṟiyūruṭai
ya mahādevar Ucciyam poḻutu Amutu ceytaruḷum potu vetam vallānoru brāhmaṇanukku Oru caṭṭi
c coṟu puṟappaṭuvataṟku patteṭṭuk kuttalarici nāḻi Uriyum ney Iru ceviṭum caṟkarai Oru ceviṭum paruppu Ā
ḻaākkum veṟuṅkāy Iraṇṭum veṟṟilai Eṭṭum kaṟi nāluncaṭṭiyum caṭṭīyiṭuṅ kucavanukkum Aṭumāṇikkum vi
ṟakiṭuvānukkumāka veṇṭum vicattukku tiruvoṟṟiyūrt tevar paṇṭārattu rājakecariyāl Ācan=tirakālam Ā
ṇṭuvarai Aḷappatāna nellu muppatiṉ kalam
jayaṅ koṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu puliyūr koṭṭattu puliyūr perumpuli
yomum
nākaccāṉi
Digital edition of SII 38.141 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
